---
title: "vanilla_gaussian_example"
output: html_document
---

## Libraries
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

set_theme(theme_bw())
```

## Setup
```{r}
# True value of phi
PHI <- 0
THETA <- 2

# Sample sizes for each data set
Y.n <- 100
Z.n <- 100

# Sample standard deviation
Y.sd <- 2
Z.sd <- 1
```

```{r}
# Density function
x <- seq(-8, 8, 0.01)
Y.density <- dnorm(x, PHI, Y.sd)
Z.density <- dnorm(x, PHI + THETA, Z.sd)

# Generate data
set.seed(123)
Y.data <- rnorm(Y.n, PHI, Y.sd)
Z.data <- rnorm(Z.n, PHI + THETA, Z.sd)

# ===== Plot the density functions and samples =====
df_chart_density <- rbind(
  data.frame(x = x, density = Y.density, dataset = "Y"),
  data.frame(x = x, density = Z.density, dataset = "Z")
)

df_chart_points <- rbind(
  data.frame(x = Y.data, dataset = "Y"),
  data.frame(x = Z.data, dataset = "Z")
)

ggplot(df_chart_density, aes(x, density, color = dataset)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(
    data = df_chart_points,
    aes(x, y = 0, color = dataset),
    alpha = 0.3
  ) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1)
```

Generate samples from the first posterior
```{r}
PHI.post.mean <- mean(Y.data)
PHI.post.sd <- Y.sd / sqrt(Y.n)

dpost_phi <- function(x) dnorm(x, PHI.post.mean, PHI.post.sd)
rpost_phi <- function(n) rnorm(n, PHI.post.mean, PHI.post.sd)
```

Conditioning on the posterior mean, generate samples from the conditional posterior of $\theta$
```{r}
dpost_theta_phi <- function(x, phi, log = FALSE) {
  post.mean <- mean(Z.data) - phi
  post.sd <- Z.sd / sqrt(Z.n)
  
  return(dnorm(x, post.mean, post.sd, log = log))
}

rpost_theta_phi <- function(n, phi) {
  post.mean <- mean(Z.data) - phi
  post.sd <- Z.sd / sqrt(Z.n)
  
  return(rnorm(n, post.mean, post.sd))
}
```

## Stage 1 Inference
For the SMC procedure, we choose the minimum value of $\varphi$ as the "representative" value and obtain samples from the conditional posterior of $\theta \mid \varphi$.

```{r}
# Obtain posterior samples from the first stage
PHI.post.samples <- rpost_phi(1000)

# Choose the minimum value as the representative value
phi.star <- min(PHI.post.samples)

# Obtain posterior samples from the conditional posterior
init_samples <- rpost_theta_phi(1000, phi.star)
phi_sequence <- sort(PHI.post.samples)
```

## Sequential Monte Carlo
```{r}
#' Multinomial Resampling
#' Keeps track of indices to avoid heavy data copying until necessary.
multinomial_resample <- function(weights, num_resamples = length(weights)) {
  sample.int(
    n = length(weights), 
    size = num_resamples, 
    replace = TRUE, 
    prob = weights
  )
}

#' Helper: Safe Log-Sum-Exp
log_sum_exp <- function(x) {
  max_x <- max(x)
  max_x + log(sum(exp(x - max_x)))
}

#' MCMC Mutation Kernel (Metropolis-Hastings) with Multiple Steps
mutate_particles <- function(samples, target_log_fn, step_size = 0.05, n_mcmc_steps = 5) {
  N <- length(samples)
  acceptances <- numeric(N) # Track how often each particle moves
  
  # Run MCMC for several steps to ensure particles reach the new target
  for (k in 1:n_mcmc_steps) {
    # 1. Propose new states
    proposals <- samples + rnorm(N, mean = 0, sd = step_size)
    
    # 2. Evaluate target density
    log_p_curr <- target_log_fn(samples)
    log_p_prop <- target_log_fn(proposals)
    
    # 3. Calculate acceptance ratios
    log_alpha <- log_p_prop - log_p_curr
    
    # 4. Accept/Reject
    log_u <- log(runif(N))
    accept_idx <- log_u < log_alpha
    
    # Update samples where accepted
    samples[accept_idx] <- proposals[accept_idx]
    
    # Track acceptances
    acceptances <- acceptances + as.integer(accept_idx)
  }
  
  # Return mutated samples and overall acceptance rate
  list(
    samples = samples, 
    accept_rate = mean(acceptances) / n_mcmc_steps
  )
}

#' Calculate Effective Sample Size (ESS)
calculate_ess <- function(normalized_weights) {
  1 / sum(normalized_weights^2)
}

#' Main SMC Sampler with ESS Tracking
smc <- function(
    init_samples,
    phi_sequence,
    step_size = 0.05,
    n_mcmc_steps = 5,
    ess_threshold_ratio = 0.5
) {
  S <- length(phi_sequence)
  N <- length(init_samples)
  
  # Trigger resample when ESS falls below this number (typically N/2)
  ess_threshold <- ess_threshold_ratio * N
  
  # Pre-allocate dynamically
  THETA_PHI <- matrix(NA, nrow = S, ncol = N)
  THETA_PHI[1, ] <- init_samples
  
  curr_samples <- init_samples
  
  # 1. Initialize cumulative log weights equally
  log_weights <- rep(-log(N), N) 
  
  for (s in 2:S) {
    log_prev_fn <- function(x) dpost_theta_phi(x, phi_sequence[s - 1], log = TRUE)
    log_curr_fn <- function(x) dpost_theta_phi(x, phi_sequence[s], log = TRUE)
    
    # --- REWEIGHT ---
    # Calculate incremental weights: log(\pi_s(x) / \pi_{s-1}(x))
    log_incremental_weights <- log_curr_fn(curr_samples) - log_prev_fn(curr_samples)
    
    # Add incremental weights to cumulative weights
    log_weights <- log_weights + log_incremental_weights
    
    # Normalize weights safely
    log_denom <- log_sum_exp(log_weights)
    normalized_weights <- exp(log_weights - log_denom)
    
    # --- CHECK ESS ---
    ess <- calculate_ess(normalized_weights)
    
    if (ess < ess_threshold) {
      # --- RESAMPLE ---
      resample_idx <- multinomial_resample(normalized_weights)
      curr_samples <- curr_samples[resample_idx]
      
      # Reset cumulative weights to uniform after resampling
      log_weights <- rep(-log(N), N)
      
      # --- MUTATE ---
      # Rejuvenate particles to recover diversity
      mutation_out <- mutate_particles(
        samples = curr_samples,
        target_log_fn = log_curr_fn,
        step_size = step_size,
        n_mcmc_steps = n_mcmc_steps
      )
      curr_samples <- mutation_out$samples
      
      message(sprintf("Step %d | Phi: %.3f | RESAMPLED (ESS: %.1f) | Accept Rate: %.1f%%", 
                      s, phi_sequence[s], ess, mutation_out$accept_rate * 100))
    } else {
      # If ESS is healthy, we just carry the particles and weights forward!
      message(sprintf("Step %d | Phi: %.3f | Skipped Resampling (ESS: %.1f)", 
                      s, phi_sequence[s], ess))
    }
    
    # Update state tracker
    THETA_PHI[s, ] <- curr_samples
  }
  
  return(THETA_PHI)
}
```

```{r}
theta_phi_samples <- smc(init_samples, phi_sequence, ess_threshold_ratio = 0.8)
```

```{r}
# 1. Prepare theoretical density data
dEP_theta <- function(x) {
  post.mean <- mean(Z.data) - mean(Y.data)
  post.sd <- Y.sd/sqrt(Y.n) + Z.sd/sqrt(Z.n)
  
  dnorm(x, post.mean, post.sd)
}

x_grid <- seq(-1, 3, length.out = 500)
df_theory <- data.frame(
  x = x_grid,
  density = dEP_theta(x_grid)
)

# 2. Prepare samples data
# Note: Apply some thinning
df_samples <- data.frame(theta = as.vector(theta_phi_samples))

# 3. Create the Density Comparison Plot
ggplot() +
  # Empirical SNIS Distribution (KDE)
  geom_density(
    data = df_samples,
    aes(x = theta, fill = "SMC"),
    alpha = 0.3,
    color = "steelblue"
  ) +
  # Theoretical Target Density
  geom_line(
    data = df_theory,
    aes(x = x, y = density, color = "Theoretical Target")
  ) +
  # Scale and Aesthetic adjustments
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Distribution", values = c("SMC" = "steelblue")) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "", values = c("Theoretical Target" = "darkred")) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", aspect.ratio = 1)
```
